title: Split task lifecycle from publish lifecycle and harden strict completion semantics
summary: Make publish intent, publish-pending, publish-failed, and publish-ack semantics explicit so strict `require` can separate logical task completion from repo publication, prevent false completion during push failures, and keep user-code commit scope manual.

acceptance.label: PRISM can represent publish-pending and publish-failed states without falsely marking a task completed before repo publication succeeds.
Publish acknowledgement and verification state live outside the repo-published projection so confirming publication does not re-dirty `.prism` artifacts or create a commit loop.

Task lifecycle and publish lifecycle are represented separately enough for `require` to track desired completion before final publication while keeping source-code commit scope with the agent or human.
